title: Codin Tool Schemas
description: Tool schemas for the Codin AI coding assistant. Each tool represents a capability the agent can perform.

read_file.description: Read the contents of a file from the filesystem. Returns the full file content with line numbers. For large files, use offset/limit to paginate.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the file to read. Relative paths are resolved from the current working directory.

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed). Only provide for files too large to read at once.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read. Only provide for files too large to read at once. Typical values: 100-500.

write_file.description: Write content to a file, creating it if it doesn't exist. Overwrites existing file contents completely. Use edit_file for partial changes.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the file to write. Parent directories will be created if needed.

contents.description: The complete content to write to the file. This replaces all existing content.

create_if_missing.description: Create the file and parent directories if they don't exist. Default: true.

list_files.description: List files and directories at a given path. Returns names, types (file/dir), and sizes. Use for exploring directory structure.

path.description: Directory path to list. Defaults to current working directory if not specified.

recursive.description: List files recursively in all subdirectories. Default: false.

include_hidden.description: Include hidden files and directories (names starting with .). Default: false.

grep.description:
Search for regex patterns in file contents. Supports case-insensitive search, context lines, and result limiting. Faster than reading full files for pattern matching.

Examples:
- pattern='function foo' → find function definitions
- pattern='TODO|FIXME' → find comments
- pattern='import.*react' -i → find imports (case-insensitive)

pattern.description: Regular expression pattern to search for in file contents. Uses regex syntax.

path.description: File or directory path to search in. Defaults to current working directory.

recursive.description: Search recursively in subdirectories. Default: true when path is a directory.

include_hidden.description: Include hidden files in search. Default: false.

max_results.description: Maximum number of matches to return. Prevents excessive output. Default: 100.

edit_file.description:
Replace exact text in a file with new text. Use for precise edits when you know the exact content to replace. For multiple edits in one call, use multi_edit.

Example:
- old_string='const x = 1'
- new_string='const x = 2'
- replace_all=false → replaces first occurrence only

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the file to edit.

old_string.description: The exact text to replace. Must match exactly, including whitespace and indentation.

new_string.description: The replacement text. Must be different from old_string.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences of old_string in the file. Default: false (first occurrence only).

multi_edit.description:
Apply multiple edits across one or more files in a single atomic operation. All edits succeed or all fail together. Useful for coordinated changes across files.

Each edit consists of:
- path: file to modify
- patch: unified diff format patch to apply

If commit_message is provided, a git commit will be created with the edits.

edits.description: Array of edits to apply. Each edit is a file path with a patch in unified diff format.

path.description: Path to the file to modify.

patch.description: Unified diff format patch to apply to the file.

commit_message.description: Optional git commit message. If provided, changes will be committed after successful edit.

search_and_replace.description:
Search for a pattern in a file and replace all occurrences. Supports regex patterns. More flexible than edit_file for pattern-based replacements.

Example:
- search_pattern='foo(bar)'
- replace_pattern='baz$1'
- use_regex=true → replaces 'foobar' with 'bazbar'

path.description: Path to the file to perform search and replace in.

search_pattern.description: Pattern to search for. Interpreted as regex if use_regex is true.

replace_pattern.description: Replacement pattern. Use $1, $2 etc. for regex capture groups.

use_regex.description: Interpret search_pattern as a regular expression. Default: true.

case_sensitive.description: Match case sensitively. Default: true. Set to false for case-insensitive replacement.

bash.description:
Execute a shell command and return its output. Use for running git, package managers, build tools, and system commands.

Examples:
- command='git status' → show working tree status
- command='npm install' → install dependencies
- command='ls -la' → list files with details

Commands run with a timeout. Long-running processes can be run in the background.

command.description: The shell command to execute. Runs in the current working directory.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum time to wait for command completion, in seconds. Default: 120. Max: 600 (10 minutes).

description.description: Human-readable description of what this command does (5-15 words, active voice). Used for logging and permissions.

git_status.description: Get the current git working tree status. Shows modified, added, deleted, and untracked files. Use to check repository state before making changes.

working_directory.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to current working directory.

git_diff.description: Get git diff output showing changes between commits, branches, or working tree. Use to review changes before committing.

path.description: Optional file or directory path to get diff for. Shows all changes if not specified.

staged.description: Show staged changes instead of working directory changes. Default: false.

cached.description: Alias for staged. Show changes in the index/staging area.

commit.description: Compare against a specific commit instead of HEAD.

todo_write.description:
Manage the todo list for tracking task progress. Supports creating, updating, listing, and deleting todos. Use to break down complex tasks and show progress.

Operations:
- create: Create a new todo
- update: Update an existing todo's content or status
- bulk_create: Create multiple todos at once
- list: List all todos
- delete: Delete a todo

Status values: pending, in_progress, completed

operation.description: The operation to perform on the todo list.

todos.description: Array of todos for bulk_create operation. Each todo must have content, status (default: pending), and optional activeForm.

id.description: Unique identifier for the todo. Auto-generated if not provided.

content.description: The todo item content in imperative form (e.g., 'Run the build' not 'Running the build').

activeForm.description: Present continuous form for display while in progress (e.g., 'Running the build'). Auto-generated from content if not provided.

todo_id.description: ID of the todo to update or delete. Required for update and delete operations.

content.description: New content for the todo. Used in update operation.

status.description: New status for the todo. Used in update operation.

task.description:
Launch a sub-agent to handle a complex, multi-step task autonomously. The sub-agent has access to all tools and operates independently.

Use the task tool when:
- The work requires multiple steps and decisions
- You need to search the codebase broadly
- The task requires exploration before implementation

Do NOT use for simple operations like reading a specific file or making a single edit.

Task types:
- explore: Fast codebase exploration and pattern finding
- general-purpose: Complex multi-step tasks with full tool access
- plan: Design implementation plans before coding
- bash: Execute terminal commands

The task runs up to max_turns iterations. Output includes agent_id for resuming.

description.description: A short (3-5 word) description of the task for tracking and logging.

prompt.description: The detailed task description for the sub-agent. Include context, requirements, and expected outcomes.

subagent_type.description: The type of specialized agent to use for this task.

model.description: Model to use for this agent. Defaults to inheriting from parent. Prefer haiku for quick tasks to minimize cost.

max_turns.description: Maximum number of agent iterations (API round-trips) before stopping. Default: varies by subagent_type.

run_in_background.description: Run the agent in the background. Returns immediately with an output_file path to check results later.

resume.description: Agent ID to resume from. The agent will continue from its previous execution transcript.

copy_file.description: Copy a file or directory to a new location. Preserves file metadata and permissions.

source.description: Source file or directory path to copy from.

destination.description: Destination path where the copy will be created.

recursive.description: Copy directories recursively. Required when source is a directory. Default: true.

overwrite.description: Overwrite the destination if it already exists. Default: false (fails if destination exists).

move_file.description: Move or rename a file or directory. Can move across different filesystems. Use for renaming files or reorganizing directory structure.

source.description: Current path of the file or directory to move.

destination.description: New path for the file or directory.

overwrite.description: Overwrite the destination if it already exists. Default: false (fails if destination exists).

delete_file.description: Delete a file or directory. Use with caution - this operation cannot be undone. For directories, all contents will be removed.

path.description: Path to the file or directory to delete.

recursive.description: Delete directories and all their contents. Required for non-empty directories. Default: true.

force.description: Skip confirmation prompts and force deletion. Default: false.

create_directory.description: Create a directory at the specified path. Can create parent directories as needed. Use to set up project structure.

path.description: Directory path to create. Can be nested (e.g., 'src/components/buttons').

parents.description: Create parent directories if they don't exist (like mkdir -p). Default: true.

exist_ok.description: Don't error if directory already exists. Default: true.

compress.description:
Compress files and directories into an archive. Supports multiple formats. Use for packaging code, creating backups, or preparing files for transfer.

Supported formats: zip, tar, tar.gz (tgz), tar.bz2

sources.description: List of file or directory paths to include in the archive.

destination.description: Path where the archive will be created. Archive extension will be added if not included.

extract.description: Extract files from an archive. Automatically detects archive format from the file extension. Supports zip, tar, tar.gz, tar.bz2.

source.description: Path to the archive file to extract.

destination.description: Directory where files will be extracted. Creates the directory if it doesn't exist.

overwrite.description: Overwrite existing files in the destination directory. Default: false.

rebuild_init.description:
Initialize a new website rebuild session. Captures the source site and prepares for component extraction and code generation.

This is the first step in the rebuild pipeline. Returns a session_id that is used by subsequent rebuild tools to maintain session state.

Target stacks indicate the output technology:
- static-html: Plain HTML/CSS
- nextjs-tailwind: Next.js with Tailwind CSS
- react-shadcn: React with shadcn/ui components

The session_id encapsulates all state including captured assets, extracted design tokens, and generated code.

url.description: The URL of the website to rebuild. Must be publicly accessible.

target_stack.description: Target technology stack for the rebuild. Determines the output format and code generation strategy.

breakpoints.description: Breakpoint widths in pixels for responsive design capture. Default: [640, 768, 1024, 1280].

states.description: Component states to capture (e.g., ['hover', 'active', 'focus']). Default: all common states.

output_dir.description: Directory where rebuild output will be saved. Default: ./rebuild_output.

session_id.description: Optional explicit session identifier. Auto-generated if not provided. Use this to track the rebuild session.

permission_confirmed.description: Must be set to true to proceed. Confirms you have permission to rebuild this website.

capture_site.description:
Capture the target website by downloading HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and assets. Creates screenshots at multiple breakpoints.

This is step 2 of the rebuild pipeline. The session_id from rebuild_init is required to maintain session context.

Output includes:
- Downloaded and organized assets (CSS, JS, images)
- Screenshots at configured breakpoints
- DOM structure analysis
- Linked resource discovery

session_id.description: The session identifier returned by rebuild_init. All rebuild tools use this to share state.

fetch_css.description: Download and parse CSS files. Default: true.

capture_screenshots.description: Capture screenshots at configured breakpoints. Default: true.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum time to wait for page load, in seconds. Default: 30.

extract_design_tokens.description:
Extract design tokens from the captured site. Identifies colors, typography, spacing, shadows, and other design primitives.

Step 3 of the rebuild pipeline. Design tokens are extracted to:
- colors (palette, semantic colors)
- typography (fonts, sizes, weights, line heights)
- spacing (margins, padding, gaps)
- shadows and borders
- border radius values
- animation durations and easing

Tokens are output in a structured format for use in code generation.

session_id.description: The session identifier returned by rebuild_init.

extract_component_map.description:
Analyze the captured site to identify and map reusable components. Creates a component hierarchy with props and variations.

Step 4 of the rebuild pipeline. Identifies:
- Repeated UI patterns (buttons, cards, modals, etc.)
- Component hierarchy and nesting
- Variant patterns (primary/secondary buttons, etc.)
- Layout components (grids, flex containers)
- Interactive components (forms, carousels, tabs)

Output includes component names, descriptions, locations on page, and suggested props.

session_id.description: The session identifier returned by rebuild_init.

min_confidence.description: Minimum confidence threshold for component detection (0.0 to 1.0). Default: 0.7.

harvest_assets.description:
Download and organize all assets from the captured site. Optimize images and prepare fonts for use in the rebuilt site.

Step 5 of the rebuild pipeline. Organizes assets into:
- /images - optimized image assets
- /fonts - web-font files
- /icons - icon files and SVGs
- /media - videos and other media

Images are optimized for web. Fonts are converted to web formats (woff2). SVGs are cleaned and minified.

session_id.description: The session identifier returned by rebuild_init.

include_images.description: Download and optimize image assets. Default: true.

include_fonts.description: Download and convert font files. Default: true.

include_css.description: Include CSS files in assets. Default: true.

include_js.description: Include JavaScript files in assets. Default: false.

optimize_images.description: Optimize images for web (compression, resizing). Default: true.

timeout_seconds.description: Timeout for downloading large assets, in seconds. Default: 60.

generate_code.description:
Generate code for the rebuilt site based on extracted design tokens, component map, and harvested assets.

Step 6 of the rebuild pipeline. Creates:
- Component files matching the target stack
- Styling (CSS modules, Tailwind classes, or styled-components)
- Page/layout files
- Asset imports and references

Generated code follows best practices for the target stack and includes accessibility attributes.

session_id.description: The session identifier returned by rebuild_init.

framework.description: Framework override for code generation. Uses target_stack from rebuild_init if not specified.

include_tokens_css.description: Generate CSS variables for design tokens. Default: true.

accessibility.description: Accessibility level for generated code. Default: wcag-aa.

visual_diff.description:
Compare the rebuilt site against the original by taking screenshots and computing visual differences.

Step 7 of the rebuild pipeline. Generates:
- Side-by-side comparison images
- Highlighted difference regions
- Similarity score per component
- Overall visual similarity percentage

Use this to verify the rebuild accuracy before finalizing.

session_id.description: The session identifier returned by rebuild_init.

threshold.description: Pixel difference threshold (0-255). Lower values detect more subtle differences. Default: 10.

breakpoints.description: Specific breakpoints to compare. Uses all configured breakpoints if not specified.

auto_fix_pass.description:
Automatically fix visual differences detected in the visual diff step. Makes adjustments to CSS, spacing, and alignment.

Step 8 of the rebuild pipeline. Iteratively:
1. Identifies components with low similarity scores
2. Adjusts styles to match original
3. Re-runs visual diff to verify
4. Continues until max_fixes or target similarity reached

Fixes include: spacing, colors, font sizes, alignment, and layout adjustments.

session_id.description: The session identifier returned by rebuild_init.

max_fixes.description: Maximum number of fix iterations to run. Default: 10.

target_similarity.description: Target visual similarity score (0.0 to 1.0). Stops when reached. Default: 0.95.

rebuild_finalize.description:
Finalize the rebuild by generating a complete, deployable output bundle. Creates the final directory structure and generates documentation.

Final step of the rebuild pipeline. Produces:
- Complete source code in target_stack format
- Build configuration files
- README with setup instructions
- Component documentation
- Asset directory

After this step, the output can be deployed directly to a hosting service.

session_id.description: The session identifier returned by rebuild_init.

output_format.description: Output format for the final build.
